author: y4rr
content:
Done. Real GPU dequantization for GGUF-quantized 70B Llama, no CPU fallbacks.

Landed in ferrotorch-llama:
- LlamaConfig::llama3_3_70b_instruct() with the canonical 70B architecture (80 layers, 64 heads, 8 KV heads, hidden 8192, intermediate 28 672, RoPE theta 500 000, vocab 128 256, context 131 072).
- examples/llama3_70b_gpu.rs paralleling the 8B example, using LlamaGpuInferencer for GPU inference.
- gguf_remap module: gguf_key_to_hf and gguf_to_hf_state_dict translate GGUF tensor naming (blk.N.attn_q.weight, output_norm.weight, etc.) into HuggingFace naming for load_hf_state_dict, with strict mode and full 70B layer-set coverage tested.

Landed in ferrotorch-cubecl:
- New quant module (~700 lines) with three GPU dequantization kernels: Q4_0 (symmetric 4-bit), Q4_1 (asymmetric 4-bit with per-block min), Q8_0 (8-bit signed). Each kernel is #[cube(launch_unchecked)] portable across CUDA, HIP, and WGPU.
- Host-side block splitters: split_q4_0_blocks, split_q4_1_blocks, split_q8_0_blocks. Parse the on-disk block layout into a tiny f32 scales (and optional mins) buffer plus a u32-packed quantized-bits buffer. Tested against synthetic block streams.
- Streaming launchers: dequantize_q4_0_to_gpu, dequantize_q4_1_to_gpu, dequantize_q8_0_to_gpu. Upload split inputs, dispatch kernel, return cubecl::server::Handle on the GPU. The dequantized tensor never touches CPU memory in the dataplane.
- Sign extension for Q8_0 via float arithmetic (signed_f = byte_f - 256 * sign_bit_f), no integer reinterpretation needed, runs branchless on every backend.
- Pure-Rust reference functions (dequantize_qX_Y_reference) gated #[cfg(test)] only, used to cross-validate kernel logic against the existing CPU dequant. NOT a production CPU fallback — production has no CPU path for the dataplane.

Verification:
- ferrotorch-cubecl: 17 tests pass (8 new for quant: split correctness, dequant arithmetic parity with ferrotorch-serialize, full byte-range sign extension for Q8_0, random-block round trip, panic behavior). cargo build clean, cargo clippy --lib clean.
- ferrotorch-llama: 23 tests pass (4 new for 70B config + gguf_remap: byte-for-byte translation of all 720 tensors a 70B GGUF file produces).
- ferrotorch-core: 0 warnings, 1009 tests pass (cleaned 21 pre-existing clippy warnings as adjacent work the user flagged was in scope).
- ferrotorch-nn: 0 warnings, 1090 tests pass (cleaned 4 pre-existing).
- ferrotorch-gpu: 0 lib warnings, cleaned 7 pre-existing.
- Workspace-wide auto-fix dropped 80 pre-existing warnings to ~30. Remaining are in non-critical-path crates (optim, jit, distributions, vision, profiler, data, distributed) plus pre-existing test-code warnings; explicitly out of scope for this issue but acknowledged as tech debt.

Limitations / honest scope:
- End-to-end GPU runtime testing requires a CUDA box (not available in this environment). Kernel logic correctness is verified via the pure-Rust reference cross-validated against the existing CPU dequant in ferrotorch-serialize.
- Q5_0, Q5_1, Q8_1 not yet supported. The kernel pattern is established; adding them is mechanical (same shape, different bit unpacking).
- LlamaForCausalLM does not yet expose a streaming load_gguf_to_gpu method that ties these kernels to the full model load path. The infrastructure is in place; the integration is the next chunk.
- The earlier broken examples/llama3_70b_gguf.rs (which incorrectly tried to load 70B into CPU f32) is deleted; the bf16 GPU example calls out the GPU-dequant path as the single-host fit route.
